title: Dubravan
content:
{{Top|fa|canon=Unidentified green-skinned species}} {{Species |image=[[File:Dubravan-VQ.jpg]] |name=Dubravan |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Vader's Quest 2|''Vader's Quest'' 2]] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=Bright [[Color/Legends|green]] |haircolor=Blond, brown, red, green, white, gray |feathers= |eyecolor=Black |distinctions=Yellow [[Blood/Legends|blood]], webbed digits |lifespan= |origin=[[Dubrava/Legends|Dubrava]]{{CSWECite|I|213|Dubrava}} |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Not that way! Not without a local guide!''"
"''I think I know where she's going, Sir!|Two stormtroopers chase the Dubravan Nevana through the swamps of her homeworld|}} '''Dubravans''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Dubrava/Legends|Dubrava]]. They possessed yellow [[Blood/Legends|blood]], black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and webbed [[Limb/Legends|hands]] and feet with four digits apiece. Anatomically, [[Gender/Legends|females]] of the species were distinguished by their [[Breast/Legends|breasts]]; however, cultural signifiers of gender included clothing and [[Hair/Legends|hair]] styles. The swamps of the species' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] supported a simple civilization based upon [[Wood/Legends|wood]] construction and [[Water/Legends|water]]-based transportation. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Dubrava became a site of conflict between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the Empire had dispatched [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to the world, while the Rebellion sent the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Jal Te Gniev]] there as a recruiter. The Rebel resented the assignment and rebuffed a young Dubravan named [[Bobek]] who wished to join the movement. However, when the Human revealed to a Dubravan female named [[Nevana]] that [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] had destroyed the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], she sold the information to a Dubravan [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Sarma]], who in turn informed the Empire. Nevana accidentally led a group of stormtroopers to the [[Unidentified bar (Dubrava)|bar]] from which Gniev operated, and in the ensuing events, Nevana and Bobek both [[Death/Legends|died]], and Gniev fled the world to warn Skywalker that the Empire was pursuing him. ==Biology and appearance== Dubravans were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] characterized by their bright-[[Color/Legends|green]] skin and smooth-featured faces. The visage of a representative of the [[Species/Legends|species]] exhibited [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like lips and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouthful]] of white [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and a pair of black, bulging [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] shaped like oblong ovals. However, despite that simplicity, the beings were capable of expressing emotions with their features, such as smiles and frowns, and the eyes were capable of producing tears. Behind the jaw at the sides of the head lay long, fleshy flaps distinguished by their deep wrinkles and squared-off shape. The smooth skin tended to wrinkle as an individual aged. A full head of [[Hair/Legends|hair]] topped the pate of most members of the species, although baldness advanced in some more aged specimens. Hair colors varied widely; blond, red, brown, green, gray, and white each manifested in various individuals. On each [[Limb/Legends|limb]], a typical Dubravan sported four webbed digits with nails. The species was divided into both [[Gender/Legends|males and females]]. Characteristics that distinguished the sexes included facial hair on some males and prominent [[Breast/Legends|breasts]] on the females. Dubravans had yellow [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Princess Leia? ''Princess Leia?'' Tell you what, if she's a princess, then the Canal District here on Dubrava is full of royalty!|Jal Te Gniev, in a barroom conversation with Dubravan patrons|}} [[File:CanalDistrict-VQ.jpg|thumb|left|250px|upright|Dubravan technology included flat-bottom boats and simple buildings made of wood.]] [[Dubrava/Legends|Dubrava]], [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Dubravans, was a swamp [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was sometimes referred to derisively as the "armpit of the universe." The world's technology was a hodgepodge of the simple and the advanced: on the one hand, individuals used such items as simple woven baskets, [[Leather/Legends|leather]] satchels, pipes with globular bowls, and metal mugs and eating utensils. On the other, [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] dishes adorned most Dubravan buildings, individuals used advanced weaponry such as [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], and some Dubravans worked at least part-time as [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Merchant/Legends|dealers]] or [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. In the planet's [[Canal District]], docks and piers connected the small islands that dotted the marshland region. Flat-bottom [[Boat/Legends|boats]] and [[Repulsorcraft/Legends|hovercraft]] moved sentient beings and goods across the [[Water/Legends|waters]] from point to point, some such craft powered by motors but others punted with poles. The islands supported Dubravan buildings, more of which were found within the confines of [[City/Legends|towns]]. The species' [[Architect/Legends|architecture]] was characterized by [[Wood/Legends|wood]]-plank construction anchored by full [[Tree/Legends|logs]] at the corners and between stories. A short flight of stairs led to the front door of a typical dwelling, and shuttered [[Window/Legends|windows]] offered another means of egress. Such buildings were relatively spartan, their coloration coming from the natural colors of the woods used to construct them. Furnishings, too, were simple, wooden affairs. Plain, dirt roads serviced traffic through the settlements. Dubravans wore gender-specific clothing akin to that prevalent among other species in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Men preferred tunics and breeches, often with a belt and [[Boot/Legends|boots]], while women took instead to simple shifts and sleek gowns with high-heeled shoes. Although males were known to wear [[Piercing/Legends|piercings]] in the fleshy growths at the sides of the head, females showed more interest in adornments such as metal [[Bracelet/Legends|bracelets]] and bangles. Hairstyle was another marker of identity. Some men wore their hair shoulder-length, while others shaved all but a shock of hair near the forehead or pulled long hair back into a ponytail. Meanwhile, women tended to keep their hair longer. Their diet included a [[Unidentified Dubrava fruit|red fruit]]. Some Dubravans enjoyed drinking frothy beverages in [[Cantina/Legends|bars]]. Another tavern pastime was card games. At least some Dubravans could speak and understand [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. ==History== [[File:Dubrava_bar_interior_VQ2.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Rebel Alliance sent Jal Te Gniev to recruit on the planet Dubrava.]] {{Quote|No! Please, no!|A Dubravan bar patron, before the establishment is torched by Imperial stormtroopers|}} The Dubravans' [[Dubrava system/Legends|home system]] received its first visitors from another world at some point between [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000]] and [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]].''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' The planet became incorporated into the greater [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] as part of the [[Triellus Trade Route/Legends|Triellus Trade Route]] and as part of [[Albanin sector/Legends|Albanin sector]],{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} located in a larger region known as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]. By [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Dubravan home [[Star system/Legends|system]] had fallen under the influence of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], a species whose domain, known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], came to encompass the area. Nevertheless, by [[14 BBY/Legends|14 BBY]], the influence had retreated. At least a few non-Dubravans traveled to the world, including a [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvian]] [[Unidentified Snivvian bartender (Dubrava)|bartender]]. At some point before [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] established a presence on Dubrava by stationing [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] there. Shorty after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] followed suit and dispatched the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Jal Te Gniev]] to Dubrava to recruit for the organization on the planet.[[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] When a young Dubravan male named [[Bobek]] sought out Gniev in a Canal District [[Unidentified bar (Dubrava)|bar]], the Human, disillusioned with his relatively backwater assignment, humiliated the boy by blindfolding him and directing him into a trap door under the ruse of running a confidence test. Gniev sent Bobek away with the aspersion that trust in the Rebellion was foolish. Incensed, Bobek left the scene, found a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], and made his way back to the bar to kill the Human pilot—as a service to the Rebellion that the young boy thought the man only nominally represented. [[File:Dubrava_firefight_VQ2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Bobek's death prompted Jal Te Gniev to warn Luke Skywalker that the Empire knew his name.]] Meanwhile, a Dubravan woman named [[Nevana]] found the inebriated Gniev at the same bar. She prodded him for information about the Rebellion and eventually coaxed the name "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Skywalker]]" from him as the identification of the [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] who had destroyed the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. However, when Nevana sold the information to a Dubravan [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter named [[Sarma]] in a nearby [[Unidentified town (Dubrava)|town]] as a way to pay off [[Nevana's father|her father's]] debt to him, Sarma merely turned her in to the Empire, although they shot him as soon as he pointed Nevana out to them. Stormtroopers ambushed the woman in the street, and she fled into the swamps. She returned to Gniev's bar and inadvertently led the stormtroopers to the Rebel recruiter's location. The Imperial [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] then burned the establishment down with [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]]; Nevana [[Death/Legends|died]] in the [[Attack on a Dubravan bar|massacre]], but Gniev escaped. Nevertheless, he found himself unarmed and facing two more stormtroopers. At that moment, Bobek, the boy whom Gniev had humiliated, appeared and shot the stormtroopers with the blaster he had intended to use to kill Gniev—although not before the Imperial [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] fatally wounded the young Dubravan. Bobek's actions inspired Gniev to rethink his role in the Rebellion and abandon his acerbic attitude.{{CSWECite|I|82|Bobek}} He bought a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] from a [[Unidentified Dubravan starfighter salesman|Dubravan salesman]] and fled the swamp planet to warn Luke Skywalker that the Empire was on his trail. Circa [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Dubrava's sentient population was between ten and one hundred million. By [[137 ABY]], Dubrava was within the territory of the [[Hutt dependencies]]. ==Behind the scenes== The [[Vader's Quest 2|second issue]] of [[Star Wars: Vader's Quest|''Vader's Quest'']], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] published in [[1999]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]], marked the first appearance of the Dubravans in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Canon|continuity]]. The green-skinned species plays a prominent role in the issue, which was written by [[Darko Macan]] and penciled by [[Dave Gibbons]]. A mistake in one frame shows the Dubravan Bobek with five fingers per hand, whereas the remainder of the comic depicts the Rebel sympathizer—along with the other members of his species—with only four digits per hand. Because the back cover of each issue of the four-part miniseries includes an illustration of how the four covers fit together to form a single image, the Dubravans Bobek and Nevana technically appear in each issue due to their prominent place on the cover of the second installment. ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', a three-volume set published by [[Del Rey]] in [[2008]], includes entries for the planet Dubrava and the characters Bobek and Nevana.{{CSWECite|II|373|Nevana}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Dubravans}} *[[Vader's Quest 1|''Vader's Quest'' 1]] {{1st}} {{C|Back cover only}} *[[Vader's Quest 2|''Vader's Quest'' 2]] *[[Vader's Quest 3|''Vader's Quest'' 3]] {{C|Back cover only}} *[[Vader's Quest 4|''Vader's Quest'' 4]] {{C|Back cover only}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|I; II||Bobek; Dubrava; Nevana}} {{1stID|Dubravan}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Dubravaner |pl=Dubravanie |pt=Dubravano }} [[Category:Outer Rim Territories sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Dubravans]] }}
